title: Ensure that the admission control plugin SecurityContextDeny is set if PodSecurityPolicy is not used

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin SecurityContextDeny is set if PodSecurityPolicy is not used
input_row.description: The SecurityContextDeny admission controller can be used to deny pods which make use of some SecurityContext fields which could allow for privilege escalation in the cluster. This should be used where PodSecurityPolicy is not in place within the cluster.
input_row.rationale: SecurityContextDeny can be used to provide a layer of security for clusters which do not have PodSecurityPolicies enabled. Impact: This admission controller should only be used where Pod Security Policies cannot be used on the cluster, as it can interact poorly with certain Pod Security Policies
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes SecurityContextDeny, if PodSecurityPolicy is not included.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --enable-admission-plugins parameter to include SecurityContextDeny, unless PodSecurityPolicy is already in place. --enable-admission-plugins=...,SecurityContextDeny,... Default Value: By default, SecurityContextDeny is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#securitycontextdeny 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/user-guide/pod-security-policy/#working-with-rbac
input_row.profile_applicability: •  Level 1 - Master Node •  Level 2 - Master Node
input_row.impact: This admission controller should only be used where Pod Security Policies cannot be used on the cluster, as it can interact poorly with certain Pod Security Policies

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_securitycontextdeny_admission_plugin_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to determine which admission plugins are enabled.
- Specifically, check the value of the --enable-admission-plugins flag in the kube-apiserver manifest (commonly located at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml).
- Validate that:
  - If the PodSecurityPolicy admission plugin is NOT present in --enable-admission-plugins, then SecurityContextDeny MUST be present.
  - If PodSecurityPolicy is present, SecurityContextDeny is not required.
- This can be automated by:
  - Using kubectl to retrieve the kube-apiserver Pod spec in the kube-system namespace (if running as a static pod):  
    ```
    kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json
    ```
  - Parsing the command line arguments from the Pod spec's containers[].command field to extract the --enable-admission-plugins value.
  - Alternatively, if direct file access is available, parse /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml for the same flag.
- Pass Criteria:
  - If --enable-admission-plugins contains SecurityContextDeny and does NOT contain PodSecurityPolicy, the check passes.
  - If --enable-admission-plugins contains PodSecurityPolicy, the check passes (SecurityContextDeny not required).
  - If neither is present, or SecurityContextDeny is missing when PodSecurityPolicy is not set, the check fails.
- Example logic:
  1. Extract the list of enabled admission plugins.
  2. If "PodSecurityPolicy" is NOT in the list, assert that "SecurityContextDeny" IS in the list.
  3. Report Pass/Fail accordingly.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.7.1 PDF
